title: మాటలు పెరిగితే — ఆపద
moral: మిత్రుల మంచి మాట వినడం ఎప్పుడూ క్షేమం, కానీ మన గర్వం మన నోరు తెరిపిస్తే ప్రమాదంలో పడతాం.
main_character: A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text:
కలువ కొలనులో కచ్ఛపం చాలా మురిసిపోయేవాడు.
ఎర్రని కలువ పూల సువాసన — మధ్యాహ్నం గాలిలో తేలి వచ్చేది.
తన స్నేహితులైన సంకటం, వికటం అనే హంసలతో గొప్పలు చెప్పేవాడు.
అతను తన ఛాతీని కొంచెం ఉబ్బుతూ, "ఈ కొలనులో ప్రతి చేప కథ నాకే తెలుసు!" అన్నాడు.
ప్రతి చిన్న విషయం కూడా తనకే తెలుసని నమ్మేవాడు.
కానీ ఈ అందమైన కొలను ఎప్పటికీ ఇలాగే ఉంటుందా...?
scenes.image_prompt: The tortoise Kachhapam happily chats with his two best friends, the swans Sankatam and Vikatam, boasting about all the secrets of the pond he knows. Character feels content and proud — puffing out his chest slightly as he tells a story. the sweet, heavy scent of blooming pink lotus flowers on the warm afternoon air. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Soft golden afternoon sunlight, warm hopeful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Kaluva Kolanulo Kachchapan Chaalaa Murisipoyevaadu.
Errani Kaluva Pula Suvaasana — Madhyaahnan Gaalilo Teli Vachchedi.
Tana Snehitulaina Sankatan, Vikatan Ane Hansalato Goppalu Cheppevaadu.
Atanu Tana Chaatini Konchen Ubbutu, "I Kolanulo Prati Chepa Katha Naake Telusu!" Annaadu.
Prati Chinna Vishayan Kudaa Tanake Telusani Nammevaadu.
Kaani I Andamaina Kolanu Eppatiki Ilaage Untundaa...?
scenes.text_en: Kachchapam was very proud in the lotus pond. The sweet scent of red lotus flowers floated in the afternoon air. He would boast to his friends, the swans Sankatam and Vikatam. Puffing out his chest a little, he said, "I know the story of every fish in this pond!" He truly believed he knew everything, even the smallest things. But would this beautiful pond always stay like this...?

scenes.text:
కొద్ది వారాలు గడిచాయి — వర్షం జాడ లేదు.
కొలను నీరు తగ్గి తగ్గి — చివరికి బురద గుంటగా మారింది.
కచ్ఛపం తన మెడ చాచి, ఇంకిపోతున్న నీటిని పదేపదే చూసేవాడు.
అతని కాళ్ళ చుట్టూ చిక్కటి బురద — ఎండి పగుళ్లుగా మారుతోంది.
అతను నిట్టూరుస్తూ, "మిత్రులారా, నీళ్ళు పూర్తిగా ఇంకిపోతున్నాయి... మన గతి ఏమిటి?" అన్నాడు.
హంసలు ఎగిరిపోగలవు, కానీ నేల మీద చిక్కుకుపోయిన తన స్నేహితుడికి ఏం జరుగుతుంది...?
scenes.image_prompt: As weeks pass with no rain, the pond shrinks into a muddy puddle, trapping Kachhapam in the sticky, hot mud. Character feels worried and anxious — constantly craning his neck to check the dropping water level. the feeling of thick, gritty mud drying and cracking around his legs. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Diffused daylight, gentle curious mood (NOT dark, NOT stormy). Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Koddi Vaaraalu Gadichaayi — Varshan Jaada Ledu.
Kolanu Niru Taggi Taggi — Chivariki Burada Guntagaa Maarindi.
Kachchapan Tana Meda Chaachi, Inkipotunna Nitini Padepade Chusevaadu.
Atani Kaalla Chuttu Chikkati Burada — Endi Pagullugaa Maarutondi.
Atanu Nitturustu, "Mitrulaaraa, Nillu Purtigaa Inkipotunnaayi... Mana Gati Emiti?" Annaadu.
Hansalu Egiripogalavu, Kaani Nela Mida Chikkukupoyina Tana Snehitudiki En Jarugutundi...?
scenes.text_en: A few weeks passed – there was no sign of rain. The pond water kept shrinking and shrinking – finally turning into a muddy pit. Kachchapam stretched his neck, looking repeatedly at the drying water. Thick mud around his legs was drying and cracking. He sighed, "Friends, the water is completely drying up... what will happen to us?" The swans could fly away, but what would happen to their friend stuck on the ground...?

scenes.text:
సంకటం, వికటం — దూరంగా ఉన్న పెద్ద సరస్సుకు వెళ్ళాలని చెప్పాయి.
వాటి రెక్కలు — శక్తివంతంగా కదిలాయి — అవి సుదీర్ఘ ప్రయాణానికి సిద్ధమయ్యాయి.
కచ్ఛపం ఒక్కసారిగా కుంగిపోయాడు.
అతను తన మెడను హంసల వైపు సాచి, దీనంగా బతిమాలాడు.
"నన్ను వదిలి వెళ్ళకండి! మీతో పాటు నన్ను కూడా తీసుకువెళ్ళండి!" అన్నాడు.
కానీ ఒక తాబేలు హంసలతో పాటు ఎగరడం ఎలా సాధ్యం...?
scenes.image_prompt: The swans announce they must fly to a larger, far-away lake, and a desperate Kachhapam begs them not to leave him behind to perish. Character feels desperate and pleading — stretching his neck out towards the swans as he speaks. the sound of the swans' powerful wings rustling as they prepare for a long flight. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Diffused daylight, gentle curious mood (NOT dark, NOT stormy). NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Sankatan, Vikatan — Durangaa Unna Pedda Sarassuku Vellaalani Cheppaayi.
Vaati Rekkalu — Shaktivantangaa Kadilaayi — Avi Sudirgha Prayaanaaniki Siddhamayyaayi.
Kachchapan Okkasaarigaa Kungipoyaadu.
Atanu Tana Medanu Hansala Vaipu Saachi, Dinangaa Batimaalaadu.
"Nannu Vadili Vellakandi! Mito Paatu Nannu Kudaa Tisukuvellandi!" Annaadu.
Kaani Oka Taabelu Hansalato Paatu Egaradan Elaa Saadhyan...?
scenes.text_en: Sankatam and Vikatam said they would go to a big lake far away. Their powerful wings moved, and they got ready for a long journey. Kachchapam's heart sank. He stretched his neck towards the swans and pleaded pitifully. "Don't leave me! Take me with you!" he cried. But how could a tortoise fly with swans...?

scenes.text:
కచ్ఛపం ఒక తెలివైన ఉపాయం చెప్పాడు.
"నేను ఒక గట్టి కర్ర మధ్యలో పట్టుకుంటాను — మీరు చివరలు పట్టుకోండి," అన్నాడు.
అతను ఒక చిన్న కొమ్మని కొరికి, తన పట్టును పరీక్షించుకున్నాడు.
బలమైన వెదురు కర్ర — అతని ముక్కుకు గట్టిగా తగిలింది.
హంసలు హెచ్చరించాయి, "గట్టిగా పట్టుకో, కానీ ఏ కారణం చేతనైనా ప్రయాణంలో నోరు తెరవకు!"
ఈ ప్రణాళిక పని చేసినా — మాటల మనిషి అయిన కచ్ఛపం నిశ్శబ్దంగా ఉండగలడా...?
scenes.image_prompt: Kachhapam proposes a clever plan: he will bite the middle of a strong stick, and the swans will carry him by holding the ends in their beaks. Character feels hopeful but nervous — testing his bite on a small twig to see if he can hold on. the hard, smooth texture of the sturdy bamboo stick against his beak. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Kachchapan Oka Telivaina Upaayan Cheppaadu.
"Nenu Oka Gatti Karra Madhyalo Pattukuntaanu — Miru Chivaralu Pattukondi," Annaadu.
Atanu Oka Chinna Kommani Koriki, Tana Pattunu Parikshinchukunnaadu.
Balamaina Veduru Karra — Atani Mukkuku Gattigaa Tagilindi.
Hansalu Hechcharinchaayi, "Gattigaa Pattuko, Kaani E Kaaranan Chetanainaa Prayaananlo Noru Teravaku!"
I Pranaalika Pani Chesinaa — Maatala Manishi Ayina Kachchapan Nishshabdangaa Undagaladaa...?
scenes.text_en: Kachchapam suggested a clever idea. "I will hold a strong stick in the middle – you hold the ends," he said. He bit a small branch and tested his grip. A strong bamboo stick fit firmly in his beak. The swans warned, "Hold on tight, but for no reason at all, open your mouth during the journey!" Even if this plan worked – could Kachchapam, a talkative creature, stay silent...?

scenes.text:
ఆకాశంలో ఎత్తుకు ఎగిరారు.
కచ్ఛపం క్రింద ఉన్న ప్రపంచాన్ని చూసి — కళ్ళు పెద్దవి చేసుకున్నాడు.
గాలి అతని శరీరంపై — ఈల వేసినట్లు దూసుకుపోయింది — ఇదివరకు ఎప్పుడూ వినని శబ్దం.
ఆ అద్భుత దృశ్యాలను చూసి నోరు తెరవకుండా ఉండడం చాలా కష్టం అయ్యింది.
అతని దవడ పట్టుకుని పట్టుకుని — నొప్పి పుట్టింది.
"కచ్ఛపం, మౌనంగా ఉండు... మనం దాదాపు చేరుకున్నాం," అని హంసలు గుర్తుచేశాయి.
ఇంతలో కింద ఒక గ్రామం వచ్చింది — మనుషులు వాళ్ళని గమనించడం మొదలుపెట్టారు.
scenes.image_prompt: High in the air, Kachhapam is amazed by the view of the world below but struggles immensely to keep his mouth shut as he sees fascinating sights. Character feels amazed but struggling with self-control — his jaw aches from clamping down so hard. the rushing wind whistling past his shell, a sound he has never heard before. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Dappled shade with quiet contemplative light. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Aakaashanlo Ettuku Egiraaru.
Kachchapan Krinda Unna Prapanchaanni Chusi — Kallu Peddavi Chesukunnaadu.
Gaali Atani Sharirampai — Ila Vesinatlu Dusukupoyindi — Idivaraku Eppudu Vinani Shabdan.
Aa Adbhuta Drrishyaalanu Chusi Noru Teravakundaa Undadan Chaalaa Kashtan Ayyindi.
Atani Davada Pattukuni Pattukuni — Noppi Puttindi.
"Kachchapan, Maunangaa Undu... Manan Daadaapu Cherukunnaan," Ani Hansalu Gurtucheshaayi.
Intalo Kinda Oka Graaman Vachchindi — Manushulu Vaallani Gamaninchadan Modalupettaaru.
scenes.text_en: They flew high in the sky. Kachchapam's eyes widened as he saw the world below. The wind rushed past his body, whistling – a sound he had never heard before. It became very difficult to keep his mouth shut seeing those amazing sights. His jaw hurt from holding on. "Kachchapam, be quiet... we are almost there," the swans reminded him. Meanwhile, a village appeared below – people started noticing them.

scenes.text:
కింద ఉన్న గ్రామస్తులు వాళ్ళని చూసి — వేళ్ళు చూపారు.
"చూడండి! ఆకాశంలో ఎగిరే మూర్ఖపు తాబేలు!" అని వాళ్ళు ఎగతాళి చేశారు.
మనుషుల నవ్వు శబ్దం — పైకి తేలి వినిపించింది.
కచ్ఛపం కళ్ళు సన్నగా చేశాడు — తన గర్వం దెబ్బతిన్నది.
హంసల హెచ్చరికను ఒక్క క్షణం మర్చిపోయాడు.
అతను కింద వాళ్ళను తిట్టడానికి — ఒక్కసారిగా నోరు తెరిచాడు.
అతను నోరు తెరిచాడు... ఇప్పుడు ఏం జరుగుతుంది...?
scenes.image_prompt: Villagers below point and laugh at the strange sight, and Kachhapam, forgetting the swans' warning in a flash of wounded pride, opens his mouth to shout back at them. Character feels angry and insulted — his eyes narrow as he hears the mocking shouts from below. the faint, mocking sound of human laughter drifting up from the ground. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Bright clear daylight, playful energetic mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Kinda Unna Graamastulu Vaallani Chusi — Vellu Chupaaru.
"Chudandi! Aakaashanlo Egire Murkhapu Taabelu!" Ani Vaallu Egataali Cheshaaru.
Manushula Navvu Shabdan — Paiki Teli Vinipinchindi.
Kachchapan Kallu Sannagaa Cheshaadu — Tana Garvan Debbatinnadi.
Hansala Hechcharikanu Okka Kshanan Marchipoyaadu.
Atanu Kinda Vaallanu Tittadaaniki — Okkasaarigaa Noru Terichaadu.
Atanu Noru Terichaadu... Ippudu En Jarugutundi...?
scenes.text_en: The villagers below saw them and pointed fingers. "Look! A foolish tortoise flying in the sky!" they mocked. The sound of people's laughter floated up and was heard. Kachchapam narrowed his eyes – his pride was hurt. He forgot the swans' warning for a moment. To scold the people below, he suddenly opened his mouth. He opened his mouth... what would happen now...?

scenes.text:
నోరు తెరవగానే — కర్ర అతని పట్టు నుండి జారింది.
ఒక్కసారిగా గాలి శబ్దం ఆగిపోయింది — భయంకరమైన నిశ్శబ్దం.
కచ్ఛపం ఆకాశం నుండి కిందకు పడ్డాడు.
అతని కళ్ళు పెద్దవిగా అయ్యాయి — భయంతో నిండిపోయాయి.
హంసలు భయంతో చూస్తూ ఉండిపోయాయి.
వాటి కేకలు గాలిలో కలిసిపోయాయి.
"అయ్యో! మిత్రమా!" అని అవి అరిచాయి.
గర్వం వల్ల కచ్ఛపం తన ప్రాణాలను కోల్పోయాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: Having let go of the stick, Kachhapam falls from the sky as his horrified friends can only watch, their cries lost in the wind. Character feels terrified and regretful — a silent, wide-eyed fall. the sudden, complete silence as the wind stops rushing past his ears. A slow-moving but very talkative tortoise named Kachhapam, with a smooth, moss-green shell and bright, curious eyes. He believes he is the wisest creature in his pond because he is a great observer, but his pride often makes him speak when he should listen. Always the same character across every scene. A once-lush lotus pond named 'Kaluva Kolanu' in the Godavari delta region of Andhra Pradesh, during a severe summer drought. The scene is dominated by the cracked, drying mud, wilting pink lotus flowers, and the shade of a large mango grove on the bank. Same location across every scene. Warm golden-hour glow, triumphant peaceful mood. Hand-painted children's storybook illustration, classic Indian Chandamama and Amar Chitra Katha style, soft watercolor textures, rounded friendly shapes, warm earth-tone palette (terracotta, cream, sage green, warm ochre), clean composition with breathing space. NOT photorealistic. NOT 3D render. NOT anime. NOT dark or gritty. NO cracked earth, NO apocalyptic atmosphere, NO aggressive poses, NO spread wings unless scene is explicitly about flying, NO menacing expressions. Characters should look gentle, friendly, and age-appropriate for children 5-10.
scenes.text_te_en:
Noru Teravagaane — Karra Atani Pattu Nundi Jaarindi.
Okkasaarigaa Gaali Shabdan Aagipoyindi — Bhayankaramaina Nishshabdan.
Kachchapan Aakaashan Nundi Kindaku Paddaadu.
Atani Kallu Peddavigaa Ayyaayi — Bhayanto Nindipoyaayi.
Hansalu Bhayanto Chustu Undipoyaayi.
Vaati Kekalu Gaalilo Kalisipoyaayi.
"Ayyo! Mitramaa!" Ani Avi Arichaayi.
Garvan Valla Kachchapan Tana Praanaalanu Kolpoyaadu.
scenes.text_en: As soon as he opened his mouth, the stick slipped from his grip. Suddenly the sound of the wind stopped – a terrifying silence. Kachchapam fell from the sky. His eyes widened, filled with fear. The swans watched in horror. Their cries faded into the wind. "Oh no! Our friend!" they cried. Because of his pride, Kachchapam lost his life.

quality_scores.notes: The story's structure, pacing, and use of child-engagement hooks are world-class. The narrative arc is perfectly executed, and the colloquial Telugu is flawless. The single most important fix is to address the moments where emotion is stated directly, particularly during the climax ('గర్వం దెబ్బతిన్నది', 'భయంతో నిండిపోయాయి'). To reach a 9+ score, these feelings must be shown through physical action or sensation—the heat rising in his face, his stomach dropping—making the tortoise's fatal mistake felt by the child, not just reported.

moral_te_en: Mitrula Manchi Maata Vinadan Eppudu Ksheman, Kaani Mana Garvan Mana Noru Teripiste Pramaadanlo Padataan.
title_en: Too Many Words, Too Much Trouble
moral_en: It's always safe to listen to good advice from friends. But if our pride makes us open our mouths, we can get into big trouble.
